items.title: Teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible

children.content: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

gallery.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

thumbnail.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

items.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

items.title: Study finds NT residents are the nation's happiest

children.content: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

image.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

items.title: 'Kick in the guts': NT veterans join calls for health funding cap removal

children.content: The federal government wants to cap access to physical and mental health supports to $5,000 a year for injured war veterans, but there are growing concerns the move will push some into "a worse-off situation".

image.altText: A group of people sitting around a table in a cafe, an older man is pointing his finger as he speaks to the group.

items.title: Jillaroos, jackaroos and an iconic event: The Brunette Races in pictures

children.content: More than 800 people from across northern Australia hit a remote outback cattle station over the weekend for the iconic annual Brunette Races.

image.altText: A cowboy on top of a brown horse against a black night sky mid air as he is bucked from his horse.

items.title: Court finds staffer's relationship with 20yo parolee 'grossly inappropriate'

children.content: An Alice Springs parole worker found in a hotel room with the 20-year-old she was responsible for supporting has avoided prison for attempting to pervert the course of justice.

image.altText: A woman outside the court hides her face behind a cardigan, supporters around her attempt to block the view with umbrellas.

items.title: NT Police says public has no need to be concerned about new officer class

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

image.altText: Police officers in uniform converse.

items.title: Housing price downturn accelerates as 'perfect storm' hits market

children.content: Australian home values record their biggest decline in more than three years as the housing market slowdown that began in Sydney and Melbourne spreads across the country.

items.title: 'Not fair and honest': Accounting firms face split after KPMG scandal

children.content: Accounting firms could be asked to split their lucrative consulting services from their audit functions and individual firm partners could face far greater scrutiny and penalties for legal breaches, under a shake up of the industry being consulted on by the federal government.

image.altText: People walk past a KPMG sign

items.title: Scientists prepare natural enemy to fight one of Australia's worst weeds

children.content: An imported plant pathogen that will spread on the wind is being readied for release against one of Australia's worst weeds.

items.title: Certain sports have a high risk of brain injury. What can you do as a parent?

children.content: When your child decides they are keen on playing sport, there are lots of factors to consider as a parent, and after recent news stories about chronic traumatic encephalopathy, keeping them safe from head injuries may be front of mind.

items.title: Renewed push to ban 'conversion practices' in Tasmania

children.content: Tasmania was the last Australian jurisdiction to decriminalise homosexuality, but since then it has gone on to pass some of the country's most inclusive legislation. There's one final frontier — conversion practices.

image.altText: A large rainbow flag is waved in the air.

items.title: The condition that causes people to get lost in their own home

children.content: Some estimates suggest up to one in 30 people may be affected by a condition that has been described as a lifelong inability to orient oneself — even in extremely familiar surroundings.

items.title: The rise of the money mule has banks and federal police on high alert

children.content: Graham Guy was the victim of a romance scam and had his bank accounts drained over several years. Then came an unusual request that unwittingly drew him into crime.

items.title: Woman 'still here to see grandkids grow' welcomes free HIV treatment in SA

children.content: When Kath Leane was diagnosed with HIV almost 40 years ago, she feared she would never see her children — let alone her grandchildren — grow up. But the antiretroviral treatment which helped make that possible is today being made free for all South Australians.

image.altText: A grandmother with long blond hair sits in an armchair in her home with a bookcase behind her

items.title: Intelligence reports Germany under threat from extremists and foreign powers

children.content: Moscow-recruited saboteurs, Chinese spies, Iran-backed Islamist militants and violent neo-Nazis are growing problems, says intelligence service BfV.

items.title: Usman Khawaja helps launch national Islamophobia awareness campaign

children.content: The retired 39-year-old, who was the first Muslim to play international cricket for Australia, says Islamophobia is on the rise, with women most vulnerable to attacks, because "hate is more prevalent that it has been before".

items.title: Australia to play Poland in Davis Cup tie in Brisbane

children.content: The countries will face off in a 2026 World Group I tie at the Pat Rafter Arena on September 19 and 20.

items.title: Moira Deeming set to be dumped as Liberal candidate after assault allegation

children.content: Controversial MP Moira Deeming is set to be deselected as a Liberal Party candidate for the November state election for refusing to apologise to partyroom colleague Matthew Guy.

items.title: Childcare centre that exposed kids to rat poison stripped of funding

items.title: Whale census records unexplained drop in sightings off WA's South West

children.content: Marine scientists have expressed concerns following a 60 per cent drop in migrating whales seen off South West WA's coastline compared to previous years.
